documentation.value: Outputs the result of an expression. Variables, function calls, and filters can be used inside.
A Twig expression: variable, function call, or expression with filters
Executes statements such as control structures, variable assignments, and block definitions.
A Twig tag like `for`, `if`, `set`, `block`, `extends`, etc.
Defines a named block that child templates can override. Used with template inheritance.
The block identifier
The default content of the block
Declares that the current template extends a parent template. Must be the first tag in the file.
The path to the parent template
Includes and renders another template within the current template.
The path to the template to include
Optional variables to pass to the included template
Optional keyword to restrict the included template to only the passed variables
Loops over each item in a sequence (array or iterable). Provides `loop` variable with index info.
The variable name for the current element
The array, hash, or iterable to loop over

signatures.label: {% if condition %}...{% elseif condition %}...{% else %}...{% endif %}

documentation.value: Conditional rendering of template content based on expression evaluation.
A Twig expression that evaluates to a truthy or falsy value
Assigns a value to a variable inside the template.
The variable name to assign to
The expression or value to assign
Defines a reusable template fragment, similar to a function. Macros have their own scope.
The macro identifier
Parameters with optional default values
Applies a filter to transform a value. Filters are separated from the variable by a pipe `|`.
The value to transform
The filter name: `upper`, `lower`, `trim`, `slice`, `sort`, `join`, `replace`, etc.
Optional arguments to pass to the filter
Prevents the Twig engine from processing the content inside the block. Outputs it as-is.
Escapes a string for safe output. Default strategy is `html`.
The string to escape
The escaping strategy: `html`, `js`, `css`, `url`, `html_attr`
Formats a date according to the given format string (uses PHP date format).
A PHP date format string, e.g. `'Y-m-d H:i:s'`
Optional timezone, e.g. `'Europe/Paris'`
Formats a string by replacing `%s` and `%d` placeholders (works like `sprintf`).
Values to substitute into the format placeholders
Returns the number of items in a sequence or the length of a string.
A string, array, or countable object
